edge_hypothesis: All 5 RSI pullback ACCEPTs use default EMA_200 as trend filter and EMA_50 as slope detector. EMA_200 adapts over weeks — many profitable ETH intermediate trends last 2-5 days and are invisible to EMA_200 because the macro trend is flat or transitioning. By using EMA_50 as trend baseline and EMA_9 as slope detector, we capture intermediate-duration momentum that develops WITHIN EMA_200 consolidation zones. This is a fundamentally different market structure: 'micro-trending within macro-consolidation.' Price above EMA_50 (short-term bullish), EMA_9 slope rising (acceleration confirmed), RSI < 35 (pullback into the intermediate trend) = entry at a short-term support level during an intermediate uptrend. EMA_200 would see this as sideways; EMA_50 sees it as trending. On ETH 1h, intermediate trends of 50-200 bars are common and invisible to EMA_200. At 8:1 and 10:1 R:R, even moderate intermediate trends should reach 16-20x ATR from the pullback entry. Invalidation: if trade count < 30, the faster EMAs produce too many conflicting signals that filter out valid entries. If PF < 1.0, intermediate trends on ETH don't persist long enough for this entry structure.

variants.name: rsi_fast_pullback_8to1_4h
variants.description: Fast RSI pullback on ETH/4h: EMA_50 trend (instead of EMA_200), EMA_9 slope (instead of EMA_50), standard RSI thresholds, 8:1 R:R. The 8:1 R:R is proven across multiple templates. This variant tests whether the fast-trend detection concept works before pushing to extreme R:R. Close > EMA_50 confirms intermediate uptrend, EMA_9 rising confirms short-term acceleration, RSI < 35 confirms pullback into support.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < rsi_pullback_8to1_default (1.442), fast trend detection adds noise not edge
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend (fast baseline) + EMA_9 slope (fast acceleration detector) — captures intermediate trends invisible to EMA_200
RSI_14 < 35 long / > 65 short — standard pullback thresholds
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R proven sweet spot

variants.name: rsi_fast_pullback_10to1_4h
variants.description: Fast RSI pullback on ETH/4h with 10:1 R:R. Same intermediate-trend detection (EMA_50 trend, EMA_9 slope) but pushed to 10:1 R:R. Tests whether intermediate trends captured by the fast-EMA approach extend far enough for 20x ATR TP. If 8:1 works and 10:1 also works, the signal quality of fast-trend detection is confirmed at extreme R:R.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 8to1 variant, intermediate trends don't extend to 10x ATR from pullback entries
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend + EMA_9 slope — fast intermediate trend detection

variants.name: rsi_fast_pullback_8to1_1h
variants.description: Fast RSI pullback on ETH/1h with 8:1 R:R. The 1h timeframe is where intermediate trends should be most visible — EMA_50 on 1h represents ~2 days of data, EMA_9 on 1h represents ~9 hours. This captures intraday-to-multiday trends that form and resolve quickly. More bars = more potential trades. The 8:1 R:R is the proven safe level for first-time template variations.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if 1h trade count > 300 with PF < 1.1, signal is too noisy on faster timeframe
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend + EMA_9 slope — 1h intermediate trend captures intraday-to-multiday moves
